summary: Large mnname decompilation PR focused on the name entry/name list menu. It replaced many placeholder asm markers in src/melee/mn/mnname.c with C, tightened mnname.h prototypes, added a text2 field to MnName_GObj, corrected gmMainLib_8015DBF4 to take an s32, and split a large mnName_803ED618 data symbol in symbols.txt into Vec3 data plus adjacent string/data symbols. The decomp.dev report credited 11 new matches and 14 partial-match improvements, raising GALE01 matched code by +3956 bytes (+0.10%). Evidence is mostly diff and bot match report; there was no PR body or human review discussion in the slice.
what_changed: Implemented or substantially decompiled many mnname functions that were previously placeholders, including CompareNameStrings, DeleteName, mnName_SortNames, mnName_80237D94, mnName_ConfirmNameDeleteInput, mnName_MainInput, mnName_80238754, mnName_80238AE0, mnName_80238C34, fn_80239574, mnName_80239878, mnName_80239A24, fn_8023A0BC, mnName_8023A290, mnName_8023A59C, mnName_8023A9B4, mnName_8023AC40, and IsNameNotAllowed.

Adjusted name-string APIs from slot/index-like parameters to char* parameters where the code compares actual string data: CompareNameStrings(char*, char*), IsNameUnique(char*), and IsNameNotAllowed(char*).

Added helper functions used for matching and readability, including GetNameCount_noinline, GetNumNameList, checkStringRest, mnName_80238754_noinline, mnName_802388D4_noinline, mnName_80238964_noinline, and mnName_FindAnimLoop.
Added mnName_NameDisplayOrder[0x78] to maintain the display/sort order for the 120-name list and implemented sorting/selection behavior around that table.
Added MnNameArchive local typedef with HSD_Joint, HSD_AnimJoint, HSD_MatAnimJoint, and HSD_ShapeAnimJoint pointers, plus extern archive/data declarations needed by mnname.c.
Expanded includes in mnname.c to cover menu, placeholder, lbarchive/lbaudio/language, OS, debug, gobj, jobj, and memory dependencies introduced by the decompiled functions.
Updated MnName_GObj in mnname.h by adding an HSD_Text* text2 field at offset 0x40.

Replaced multiple UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes in mnname.h with concrete return/argument types such as u8 mnName_80237D94(s32, u8), HSD_GObj* mnName_8023A59C(u8), s32 mnName_8023AC40(void), and void/function-specific HSD_GObj* signatures.
Changed gmMainLib_8015DBF4 declaration from UNK_PARAMS to gmMainLib_8015DBF4(s32), matching its call with the deleted name index.

Corrected config/GALE01/symbols.txt by shrinking mnName_803ED618 from a single 0x440 object to a 0x18 object and adding adjacent symbols mnName_803ED630, mnName_803ED644, mnName_803ED650, mnName_803ED668, and mnName_803ED672.
smart_moves: Used targeted noinline wrapper helpers to preserve call/codegen behavior while still allowing higher-level C around GetNameCount and animation helper calls.
Split the previously oversized mnName_803ED618 symbol into smaller typed data/string symbols, which improved data matching and made later references more precise.
Moved comparison-related APIs to char* parameters once the implementation showed they operate on name strings rather than numeric slots.
Modeled the name list display order explicitly with a 0x78-byte table, matching the fixed 120-entry persistent name capacity used throughout the code.
Kept many still-unknown functions under address-style names while only improving signatures/types that were evidenced by the decompiled call sites.
Added the exact struct field needed by the new code, MnName_GObj.text2, instead of using raw offset access throughout the implementation.
decomp_lessons: When a menu file has many near-matches, small helpers such as noinline wrappers and inline comparison/count functions can be the difference between high similarity and exact matches.
Header prototypes should be tightened as soon as call sites prove concrete argument types; replacing UNK_PARAMS with HSD_GObj*, u8, s32, char*, or void improves both matching and future decomp work.

String comparison code in Melee name-entry logic uses explicit terminator globals and can treat repeated terminator-like byte pairs specially; preserve the original termination behavior instead of substituting standard strcmp semantics.
Persistent name-list operations rely on the fixed 0x78 capacity; matching code should retain the observed loop bounds and u8 casts rather than abstracting them away prematurely.
Data-symbol boundaries matter: a large anonymous data object may hide multiple strings and typed arrays, and splitting it can unlock both data and code matches.
Menu input decompilation often depends on correctly modeling shared menu globals such as mn_804A04F0, cooldown state, hovered/confirmed selections, and gobj fields like gx_link and p_priority.
naming_conventions: Address-style unknown names remain in mnName_802xxxxx/fn_802xxxxx form when semantics are not yet named.
Known public-facing helpers use descriptive PascalCase-style names already present in the module, such as GetNameText, GetNameCount, IsNameListFull, IsNameUnique, DeleteName, and IsNameNotAllowed.
Matching-only helpers use a _noinline suffix, e.g. GetNameCount_noinline, mnName_80238754_noinline, mnName_802388D4_noinline, and mnName_80238964_noinline.
Module-local data follows the mnName_ prefix and either address-style names for unknowns or descriptive names where semantics are clear, e.g. mnName_NameDisplayOrder and mnName_StringTerminator.
The new local archive typedef is named MnNameArchive and mirrors HSD resource pointer field names: joint, anim_joint, matanim_joint, and shapeanim_joint.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced noinline wrapper functions around GetNameCount and several mnName helpers to influence call generation and preserve expected compiler structure.
Used inline helpers for repeated logic, including GetNumNameList, checkStringRest, and mnName_FindAnimLoop, while keeping emitted code close to the original.
Retained explicit casts to u8, s8, s32, and pointer arithmetic in name-list loops and comparisons to match signedness and indexing behavior.
Used PAD_STACK markers in input functions where stack layout needed preservation.
Represented menu state through existing HSD_GObj fields such as gx_link and p_priority, matching observed field usage rather than inventing new high-level abstractions.
Adjusted symbol-map object sizes and string annotations to align the .data region with actual references.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increasing to 60.49%, a +0.10% / +3956 byte improvement.

The bot reported 11 new 100% matches: fn_80239574, mnName_80237D94, fn_8023A0BC, mnName_80239878, DeleteName, mnName_80238754, mnName_80238AE0, mnName_8023749C, IsNameListFull, mnName_GetColumnCount, and mnName_80238A04.

The bot reported 14 partial-match improvements, including mnName_80238C34 to 89.27%, mnName_80239A24 to 89.55%, mnName_8023A59C to 99.30%, mnName_MainInput to 91.90%, mnName_SortNames to 89.02%, CompareNameStrings to 95.71%, plus .data/.bss/.sdata2/.sdata improvements.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename address-style mnName_802xxxxx functions from this PR to semantic names.
Search for follow-up work on remaining partial matches in mnName_80238C34, mnName_80239A24, mnName_8023AC40, mnName_MainInput, and mnName_SortNames.
Check whether MnName_GObj.text2 and MnNameArchive were later moved to broader headers or refined with more complete struct definitions.
Search gmMainLib_8015DBF4 call sites to verify whether its s32 argument was later renamed or its return type resolved.
